$comment: azure-mi-ai-dba-demo — MCP server config EXAMPLE. Copy to mcp.config.json (git-ignored) and fill via env vars. NO secrets/connection strings hardcoded. Principle: READ-ONLY diagnostics; state changes require human approval via a separate path.
$mssql_primary_path: PRIMARY (recommended) path for DB metadata/DMV is the official VS Code 'SQL Server (mssql)' extension in GitHub Copilot AGENT MODE. The extension ships its own built-in MCP tools, so NO separate npx package is needed: create an Azure SQL MI connection profile with Microsoft Entra auth and query DMVs/metadata read-only from agent mode. The 'mssql' server below is the SECONDARY, headless/agent option (a community npx MCP server) for environments without the extension.
$version_note: Package versions move fast — RE-CONFIRM the latest before a demo (e.g. `npm view mssql-mcp-node version`). Verified at authoring time: mssql-mcp-node=3.0.0, @connorbritain/mssql-mcp-server=0.6.0, @azure/mcp=3.0.0-beta.22. The previously referenced '@microsoft/mssql-mcp' does NOT exist on npm (404) and was removed.

mssql.$comment: SECONDARY (headless/agent) option: community npx MCP server. This example uses 'mssql-mcp-node' (read-only BY DEFAULT — do NOT set MSSQL_ENABLE_WRITES). For Entra/AAD-only auth without a SQL password, swap to '@connorbritain/mssql-mcp-server' (SQL_AUTH_MODE=aad). All values injected via env — never hardcode secrets. Confirm env var names against the package's current docs before use.

azure.$comment: Azure MCP (@azure/mcp — exists on npm, e.g. 3.0.0-beta.x) for Log Analytics, Defender for SQL, resource metadata. Auth via az login / DefaultAzureCredential (no secrets in config). Docs: https://github.com/Azure/azure-mcp . Usage: npx -y @azure/mcp

policy.notes: Diagnosis/inspection is read-only. Any DDL/DML (index create, plan guide, config change) is proposed as a script and applied only after explicit human approval.
